name: La Ametlla de Mar and Terres de l'Ebre
name_original: La Ametlla de Mar y Terres de l'Ebre
description:
Are you up for a swim in the Mediterranean after traveling more than 80 kilometers and climbing nearly 1,100 meters along unknown roads, maritime mountain ranges and accompanying the Ebro on its way to the sea? Put the swimsuit in one of the pockets of your jersey and continue reading.

We propose a circular route that leaves from Ametlla de Mar beach itself and takes you to Terres de l'Ebre. The route has a good climb, worthy of cyclists who enjoy sporting challenges. The Gavadà road is a very little traveled road that takes you through an almost virgin mountain landscape, to this abandoned medieval town.

From here you begin the descent towards the bank of the Ebro that takes you to Ginestar, where the landscape changes completely from the views of the mountain to the views of the river, its bank and the towns that are on the other side, such as Benissanet or Miravet, with its castle that appears between the treetops.

Before returning to Ametlla, you have another section of lower mountain through the Sierra del Cardó, passing through Perelló and continuing towards the sea among endless olive groves. Back on the beach, a dip is the perfect culmination of this adventure.
region_intro: Road cycling routes around L'Ametlla De Mar traverse a diverse landscape, ranging from the Mediterranean coastline to inland mountainous areas. The region features quiet, well-maintained roads that wind through flat plains, such as the Ebro Delta, and ascend towards challenging climbs in the Sierra de Cardó and Serra de Llaberia. Cyclists encounter a mix of coastal views, agricultural lands, and forested hills, providing varied terrain for different preferences. The area's natural features include numerous bays, beaches, and significant elevation changes as routes move inland. Best road cycling routes around L'Ametlla De Mar The most popular road cycling route is Montañas de Tivissa-Vandellòs – return from l'Ametlla de Mar by the collado de Fatxes, a 41.0 miles (65.9 km) trail that takes 3 hours 20 minutes to complete. This difficult route explores mountainous terrain and includes the Collado de Fatxes. Another top favourite among local road cyclists is La Ametlla de Mar and Terres de l'Ebre, a difficult 50.5 miles (81.2 km) path. This route covers the wider Terres de l'Ebre region, offering diverse landscapes and significant elevation gain. Local road cyclists also love the Riudecanyes Reservoir – Barranc de la Dòvia loop from l'Ametlla de Mar, a 66.6 miles (107.3 km) trail leading through reservoir areas and ravines, often completed in about 5 hours 8 minutes. Road cycling around L'Ametlla De Mar is defined by coastal roads, flat plains, and mountainous ascents, with quiet roads offering scenic views. The network offers options for different ability levels, from moderate coastal spins to demanding climbs. The routes in L'Ametlla De Mar are highly rated by the komoot community with an average score of 4.5 stars from over 30 reviews. More than 2,300 road cyclists have used komoot to explore L'Ametlla De Mar's varied terrain.
region_faq:
Q: How many road cycling routes are available around L'Ametlla de Mar?
A: There are over 50 road cycling routes around L'Ametlla de Mar, offering a diverse range of experiences. These routes are highly rated by the komoot community, with an average score of 4.5 stars, and have been explored by more than 2,300 road cyclists.

Q: Are there easy, flat road cycling routes suitable for beginners in L'Ametlla de Mar?
A: Yes, L'Ametlla de Mar offers several easier routes, with 3 specifically categorized as easy. The region's diverse terrain includes flat plains and coastal paths, which are ideal for a more relaxed pace. These routes often provide scenic views without significant elevation challenges.

Q: Where can I find challenging road cycling routes near L'Ametlla de Mar?
A: For experienced cyclists seeking a challenge, L'Ametlla de Mar provides 16 difficult routes that venture into mountainous areas. You can explore demanding climbs in the &lt;strong&gt;Sierra de Cardó&lt;/strong&gt;, &lt;strong&gt;Serra de Llaberia&lt;/strong&gt;, and the &lt;strong&gt;Prades Mountains&lt;/strong&gt;. Routes like &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e970501336/montanas-de-tivissa-vandellos-return-from-l-ametlla-de-mar-by-the-collado-de-fatxes" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Montañas de Tivissa-Vandellòs – return from l'Ametlla de Mar by the collado de Fatxes&lt;/a&gt; and &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1350090500/la-ametlla-de-mar-and-terres-de-l-ebre" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;La Ametlla de Mar and Terres de l'Ebre&lt;/a&gt; offer significant elevation gains and explore the wider Terres de l'Ebre region.

Q: What is the best time of year for road cycling in L'Ametlla de Mar?
A: The Mediterranean climate of the Costa Daurada makes L'Ametlla de Mar an excellent cycling destination from &lt;strong&gt;February to May&lt;/strong&gt; and in &lt;strong&gt;early autumn&lt;/strong&gt;. During these periods, you can expect mild winters and favorable weather conditions, with daytime temperatures typically ranging between 12 and 16 degrees Celsius on the coast and minimal rainy days.

Q: Are there family-friendly road cycling routes in L'Ametlla de Mar?
A: While specific family-friendly road cycling routes are not explicitly highlighted, the region's easy and moderate coastal paths and flat plains offer suitable options for families. For a very relaxed experience, the &lt;strong&gt;Via Verda&lt;/strong&gt; (Greenway) in Terres de l'Ebre, which follows old railway tracks, provides a beautiful, largely descending path through natural areas, though it's important to check if sections are paved for road bikes.

Q: Are there circular road cycling routes available around L'Ametlla de Mar?
A: Yes, many road cycling routes in the area are designed as loops, allowing you to start and finish in the same location. An example is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/39575486" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Riudecanyes Reservoir – Barranc de la Dòvia loop from l'Ametlla de Mar&lt;/a&gt;, which takes you through reservoir areas and ravines.

Q: What kind of landmarks and natural features can I see along the road cycling routes?
A: Road cycling routes around L'Ametlla de Mar offer a variety of scenic landmarks and natural features. You can encounter coastal beauty with numerous bays and beaches, and venture inland to see mountain ranges like the &lt;strong&gt;Sierra de Cardó&lt;/strong&gt; and &lt;strong&gt;Serra de Llaberia&lt;/strong&gt;. Notable highlights include the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/802480" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Fangar Lighthouse&lt;/a&gt;, the picturesque &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/566134" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;L'Illot Rock and Beach&lt;/a&gt;, and the charming &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5247971" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Llop Marí Cove and Cave&lt;/a&gt;. You might also pass viewpoints such as &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5554769" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;View of Tossal de Montagut and Surrounding Peaks&lt;/a&gt;.

Q: Are there any specific permits required for cycling in natural parks like the Ebro Delta?
A: While general road cycling on public roads typically doesn't require permits, specific regulations might apply when entering protected natural areas like the &lt;strong&gt;Ebro Delta Natural Park&lt;/strong&gt;. It's advisable to check with local authorities or the park's official website for any specific cycling permits or restrictions that may be in place for certain trails or zones within the park.

Q: What are the road conditions like for cycling in L'Ametlla de Mar?
A: The Costa Daurada, including the area around L'Ametlla de Mar, is known for its extensive network of &lt;strong&gt;quiet, winding roads with low traffic density&lt;/strong&gt;. These roads are generally well-maintained, providing a pleasant and safe cycling experience. They often feature stunning views, traversing between valleys, mountain passes, and coastal stretches.

Q: Can I combine road cycling with cultural exploration in L'Ametlla de Mar?
A: Absolutely. Many road cycling routes pass through charming, unspoiled inland villages, offering a glimpse into authentic Catalonia with its narrow streets, stone squares, and ancient churches. The nearby city of Tarragona, a former Roman city, also provides significant cultural attractions that can be visited as part of a cycling tour or as a separate excursion.

Q: Are there any routes that offer views of the sea?
A: Yes, L'Ametlla de Mar is a traditional Mediterranean fishing village with a 16-kilometer coastline. Many routes incorporate these picturesque coastal paths, allowing cyclists to enjoy stunning sea views, especially along the numerous bays and beaches. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/17513147" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Circuit de Calafat – Coll de Balaguer loop from l'Ametlla de Mar&lt;/a&gt; is an example of a moderate route that stays closer to the coast.

Q: What do other road cyclists enjoy the most about road cycling in L'Ametlla de Mar?
A: The area is highly rated by the komoot community, with an average score of 4.5 stars. Reviewers often praise the &lt;strong&gt;diverse terrain&lt;/strong&gt;, which offers everything from gentle coastal spins to demanding climbs, and the &lt;strong&gt;quiet, scenic roads&lt;/strong&gt; that wind through varied landscapes. The combination of coastal beauty and challenging inland ascents is a particular highlight for many.
tips: possibly the quietest road in the Tarragona province, plenty of hairpins and up-and-downs. Surface is not always the smoothest, but is paved all the way

Ginestar is a town on the banks of the Ebro with a magnificent atmosphere and ideal for making a stop along the route and letting yourself be enchanted by its products, which are ideal for replenishing your strength.

In the lowlands of the Ebro depression you can see the fertility of the land despite its dry appearance. Especially the olive groves, between dry stone walls, give a Mediterranean air to the landscape.

La Ametlla de Mar is a beautiful fishing village in the Bajo Ebro region. This town is ideal to start and finish any cycling route and liven up the day with a swim in the Mediterranean and enjoy its cuisine.
